title: Bank Reconciliation — The Entries
event: From Reyes Co.'s December 31 bank reconciliation: the bank collected a $1,600 note plus $50 interest, charged a $45 service fee, and returned a customer's $380 NSF check. Prepare the required journal entries.

lines.label: note + interest
lines.why: The bank already put this in your account — the books catch up now.
lines.trap: Book-side items ONLY get entries. Deposits in transit and outstanding checks need nothing — the books already have them.

lines.label: principal collected
lines.why: The note comes off the books — it's been collected.
lines.trap: Split the credit: principal to the note, interest to its own account.

lines.label: the $50 earned
lines.trap: Forgetting this line leaves the entry unbalanced by exactly the interest — a dead giveaway.

lines.label: bank fee
lines.why: The fee is an expense the statement just revealed.
lines.trap: Small, but skipping it leaves book cash overstated by $45.

lines.label: fee taken by bank
lines.why: Already gone from the bank account; now gone from the books.
lines.trap: Credit Cash — the bank already took it; you're recording, not paying.

lines.label: customer still owes
lines.why: The customer's check bounced — they still owe you, so the receivable comes BACK.
lines.trap: Not an expense (yet) — the debt is alive again and collectible.

lines.label: deposit reversed
lines.why: The deposit you thought you had gets backed out.
lines.trap: NSF reduces YOUR cash even though the failure was the customer's — their check, your problem.

memorize.body: Entries come from the BOOK side of the reconciliation only. Bank-side items (DIT, outstanding checks) fix themselves with time.
Net effect on Cash here: +1,650 − 45 − 380 = +1,225 — exactly the book-side adjustment from the reconciliation. Use that as a self-check.

questions.prompt: Which reconciliation items require journal entries: the net cash adjustment from them = ?

distractors.feedback: You journalized the BANK-side items (1,200 DIT − 2,150 outstanding). Those never get entries — only book-side items do: +1,650 − 45 − 380 = +1,225.
That's the note alone. The service charge and NSF check also need entries: net +1,225.

build.scaffold: Three entries, all book-side. Ask of each reconciliation item: do the books already know? If yes, no entry.
